content:
To fix a misplaced modifier, reposition it so that it's next to the word intended to modify. Sometimes, you may need to insert additional words to clarify the sentence's meaning.

A better sentence would be: The dog chased the ball, running down the street.

To see more common grammatical errors, read the infographic below. These errors often happen!



(Source: https://www.grammarcheck.net/writing-mistakes-social-media/)Links to an external site.

Long Description

After you finish this page, click Next to continue to Specifics of Grammar: Dangling Modifiers.


Week 4 | Specifics of Grammar: Dangling Modifiers

Dangling Modifiers
Dangling modifiers are words or phrases that change a word, phrase, or clause not clearly stated in the sentence. This often happens when the sentence's subject is changed or when a pronoun refers to a previously mentioned noun.

An example might be: After finishing my presentation, the applause filled the room.
